node.text: In the latest episode of The Prompt, Trevor Noah stays in Seattle to chat with psychologist Dr. Jonathan Bricker about how AI chatbots are helping fight addiction.

What can we learn from nature's 3.8 billion years of experience? What does algae have to do with construction? How can spider webs influence window glass to be made safer for birds? 🕸️🦉 Microsoft has a vision for future datacenters, and they exist in harmony with nature. Read the full story on Unlocked. Link in bio.
Summarizing a 64 email thread in Outlook makes Copilot the real MVP 😭🏆

node.text: Our Chief Questions Officer visits the Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center in Seattle to learn how AI is helping doctors improve the way they detect, diagnose, and rule out false positives for breast cancer.
These world-class athletes are turning to the power of AI and data to improve their well-being, performance, and careers. Check out our Story for more. 🥇 @ParityNow

Happy Pride! 🌈

Join us in celebrating the beauty and power of the LGBTQIA+ communities @ the link in bio. #Pride

node.text:
EP 03: The Reheat With Keith Lee

Upon experiencing the Keith Lee effect, @mondaynola was searching for ways to streamline operations across the business. In partnership with Microsoft, Keith introduced the team to tools that can help redefine efficiency.

EP 02: The Reheat With Keith Lee

Becoming Keith Lee-approved was a win, but Chef Jessie of @brunchaholics_ quickly realized he needed a better way to stay organized. We introduced him to Copilot to help simplify his workflow.

node.text:
EP 01: The Reheat With Keith Lee 

After a review from Keith Lee helped @theprettylittlekitchen take off, Chef B experienced a roller coaster of emotions adjusting to overnight success. So Keith came back to make sure Chef B had what she needed to take her business to the next level.

node.text: 📍 Trevor's next stop: India. Here he explores how technology and a heat map work together in helping build long term resiliency against climate change.

Microsoft 🤝 @keith_lee125. Tune in on 6/5.

Copilot in PowerPoint is my passion ✨

node.text:
#MicrosoftPartner #Sponsored Did you know Microsoft surveys and studies thousands of employees and leaders globally every year to identify the trends that are reshaping the way we all work? The fourth annual #WorkTrendIndex report from Microsoft and LinkedIn came out this month—a survey of 31,000 people in 31 countries, labor and hiring trends in the LinkedIn Economic Graph, and data on how much time people spend in emails, meetings, and chats across M365 apps. This year’s report builds on what we learned last year as it relates to AI. See link in bio for the full report.
 
Many of the findings focus on how AI can transform our work—and specifically, how it can help us perform and feel our best at work. The report found AI has the potential to help pinpoint priorities and manage the “digital debt” that plagues our desks and inboxes—so we can spend our time on the work that’s meaningful and motivating to us. Some key findings below:
- Employees point to many benefits of AI: it helps them save time (90%) and focus on their most important work (85%) be more creative (84%).
- Digital debt continues, and employees are spending more time communicating (60% of their time) than creating (40% of their time). Many employees are turning to AI to help minimize meetings and eliminate emails—so they can spend more time ideating and innovating.
- 68% of employees say they’re struggling with the pace and volume of work, and 46% report feeling burned out. Power users say AI can help with that. They say it makes workloads more manageable (92%)—and they feel better all around: they’re more motivated (91%) and enjoy work more (91%)
 
***
 
#artificialintelligence #artificialintelligenceai #productivitytips #timemanagementtips #todolists 
#emails #meetings #innovators #creating #burnout #burnoutprevention #meaningfulwork #workmotivation #wellbeingatwork #workplacewellbeing #mentalhealth #worklife #workpsychology #organizationalbehavior #worklife #doodleart #researchdoodles #microsoft
Have you updated your @linkedin profile recently? #WorkTrendIndex

node.text:
That’s a wrap on #MSBuild! 🎉 From making AI real, to being close to the action of the opening keynote,
to learning about the innovative work that will transform how the world looks, the possibilities are endless. What’s next?

📸 Thanks to Sandra P., Vanesa C., Karen N., Jack R., Scott H., Alan S., Harald K., Katie L., Caleb J., and Gretchen G.

#MicrosoftLife #PeopleOfMicrosoft

Description: A montage of scenes at Microsoft Build, including various employees, a large ceiling structure, a person holding signage that reads Aspire, a table of food, and devices with a sign that reads NEW Surface Copilot+ PCs for developers.

node.text: Every song tells a story, and visuals can help bring them to life. With Microsoft Copilot and the help of visual artist @SamCannon and Junia, twin sister & creative director, @laufey discovered inspiration for on-stage visuals at one of her recent shows.

node.text: A new AI era begins. Introducing the fastest, most intelligent Windows PCs ever. #CopilotPCs
Let’s build. Happy 15th anniversary @minecraft 🎉

node.text: We’re excited to launch the latest edition of our Environmental Sustainability Report! 🌎⭐️ By 2030, we have committed to be a carbon negative, water positive, zero waste company that protects ecosystems. Check out the link in the bio to learn more about our approach to advancing sustainability, our progress on our commitments, and what we are learning on the journey.
Slide 3 is how I feel after Copilot turns my chaotic notes into an organized to-do list in Word.

node.text: Overwhelmed by endless emails, pings, and meetings, 75% of knowledge workers around the world are using AI to simplify their day. Are you? 👀 Findings from Microsoft and @linkedin’s 4th annual Work Trend Index are now available at the link in bio.
We’re going To The Moon with @meghantrainor. 🌙💫 Her new music video dropped today with a special guest appearance 🚀

Scroll through Work Trend Index headlines since 2021.

Every year Microsoft releases the Work Trend Index -- a report that collects data from 31,000 people across 31 countries via global, industry-spanning surveys, hiring and labor data from @linkedin, telemetry data, as well as observational studies. Each year it shines a new light on evolving trends. What does 2024 have in store for us? This year’s #WorkTrendIndex drops tomorrow. 👀

node.text: We got the team together for a Microsoft Flight Simulator landing challenge. 10/10 would do again.
The result of 1 prompt: 16 different penguins. 🐧 Create your own with Microsoft Designer.

node.text: @Xbox has teamed up with Ukie’s Digital Schoolhouse to launch Beyond Xbox: Field Trips, a series of interactive learning experiences to help young people learn through play.
The bees are buzzin'. 🐝 Reimagine action at link in bio.

🌿🎤🔍 The 1-2-3's of Bioacoustics: 1. Record the hidden symphonies of nature. 2. Use AI to delve into hours of sound, identifying each whisper and roar. 3. Decipher sound patterns to unveil the mysteries of animal behavior. 
 
Learn more about how scientists are using AI and bioacoustics to quickly identify animals in the Amazon at the link in bio. 
 
Description: Clips of the bioacoustics process, including recording sounds in the rainforest, identifying the data, and connecting it to a colorful macaw.

node.text: POV: the personality hire is leading a meeting.
Managing the sustainability of our operations is at the core of our journey to create a more sustainable world. Swipe through to learn more and reimagine action at link in bio.

node.text: Celebrating World Art Day with WordArt. 🎨
Explore new worlds and an abstract spin on nature with these images we created using @msft365designer. We shared each image as a wallpaper on our Story, so you can save the magic. ✨

We partnered with Beyond the Ball, NBA and the @nbagleague to host youth at the Sneakerverse Pl[AI]ground shoe design competition - students were invited to create "Out of this World" sneaker designs using Microsoft Copilot. 🔥
We think the result is out of this world! Made using Designer. 🌌

node.text: With @microsoftcopilot, you no longer have to awkwardly ask your coworker what that meeting is about.
Lay your cards down and pick your largest cowboy hat up tonight. Made using @msft365designer. 🐝🤠
Ready for the total solar eclipse? Swipe through for a kit of supplies. 🔭

node.text:
Our mementos can mark waypoints along our path. Christina’s cherished keepsakes guide us through her journey in STEM and gaming. What’s an important memento for you?

Visit the link in our bio to learn more about #InclusionIsInnovation.

#PeopleOfMicrosoft #DiversityAndInclusion

node.text:
Remote or in office—which one is calling you?

In-office spaces featured are at Microsoft HQ in Redmond, US. 

Because flexibility matters

#MicrosoftLife #Microsoft #HybridWork #FlexibleWork 

Description: 5 different workspaces shown, including 3 Microsoft office settings and 2 work from home settings

node.text: The Coaching by Copilot feature reviews your email and offers personalized recommendations, giving you flexibility to choose which suggestions you toss or keep. Even better? It'll customize the suggestions to better align with your unique voice and tone (to keep you from sounding like a robot).

node.text:
Our possessions can tell a story of who we are. Melissa shares a few items that reflect the heritage, achievements, and bonds that shape her. What item would you share? 
 
Visit the link in our bio for more #InclusionIsInnovation
 
#PeopleOfMicrosoft #DiversityAndInclusion

node.text: Journey into Coretta Scott King’s inspiring legacy. Together with @Microsoft, we’ve created an immersive gallery experience that chronicles her enduring legacy. #DearCoretta #CorettaScottKing

From pixels to purpose, Albert Dankwa’s Bronx roots fuel his mission to make gaming inclusive for all. Now he’s changing the game. 🎮

Link in bio for Albert’s story and more.
 
#InclusionIsInnovation #PeopleOfMicrosoft #MicrosoftLife #DiversityAndInclusion
Meet the big cheese in town (literally). 🧀 Guess the Microsoft Designer prompt we used to generate this picture and drop it in the comments.

node.text: When I’m not DJ’ing at events or creating content… I work at Xbox 🎮  Managing everything in my life can be challenging, but my secret is maintaining a clean space for creativity & productivity.  Do yourself a favor and tidy up that desk and your whole self will thank you. #CleanOffYourDeskDay

node.text:
Mindfulness is 🗝️ for Miri’s personal and work goals.

#PeopleOfMicrosoft #Copilot #Mindfulness
Peeking the night of December 14th, we asked Copilot how and where to catch the Geminid meteor shower. Who would you bring to this cosmic event? Tag them below. 🌌

What does it mean to be a dreamer? For Gonçalo, who lives with cerebral palsy, it means never feeling limited by his condition and striving to support others.

Discover his story and more by visiting the link in our bio.

#InclusionIsInnovation #PeopleOfMicrosoft #MicrosoftLife #DiversityAndInclusion #DisabilityInclusion

node.text: Live from the Teen Vogue Summit, Ava Amini, senior researcher at @Microsoft, shared 3 things everyone should know about AI! Learn more about how to use AI and where the technology is going at the link in bio.
